academic_requirements: Bachelor's degree (Laurea Triennale) or an equivalent foreign qualification. Candidates need prior knowledge in economics, science or related disciplines, including basic microeconomics and macroeconomics, statistics, general biology and chemistry, and general geology. Specific minimum CFU thresholds across the relevant SECS-P, SECS-S, MAT, BIO, CHIM and GEO scientific-disciplinary sectors are set in the official Annex / Manifesto for class LM-76; verification of personal preparation by the Admission Committee is also required.
language_requirements: Minimum English proficiency at CEFR level B2. Accepted certifications follow the UNIMIB-wide recognised-certification list (IELTS, TOEFL iBT, Cambridge, Trinity ISE and others). Candidates who do not hold a valid certificate can sit the entry English test organised by the University.
entry_exam_or_test: Open with entry requirements examination: the Admission Committee verifies curricular requirements and personal preparation through the documents submitted on apply.unimib.it (and Segreterie OnLine for Italian-degree candidates). An interview may be required where preparation is not adequately documented.
application_deadline_eu: 2026/27: 1st call 10 February 2026 – 31 March 2026; 2nd call 15 April 2026 – 6 July 2026; 3rd call 7 July 2026 – 7 September 2026 (EU citizens and non-EU citizens already residing in Italy).
application_deadline_non_eu: Non-EU candidates requiring an entry visa must apply in the 1st call (10 February – 31 March 2026) via apply.unimib.it to allow time for visa procedures. Arrival in Italy by 31 January 2027 and document verification by 28 February 2027.
required_documents: Bachelor's degree certificate, full transcript of records with CFU/ECTS and grades, curriculum vitae, motivation letter, English language certification (B2+) or equivalent proof, valid passport / ID, and, for foreign qualifications, Diploma Supplement / Statement of Comparability / Declaration of Value as required by apply.unimib.it.

source_quotes.quote: Teaching language: English; Campus: Milan; Degree class: LM-76; Admission type: open with entry requirements examination.

Admission requires prior knowledge in economics, science, or related disciplines, ensuring students have a strong foundation in fields like basic microeconomics and macroeconomics, statistics, general biology and chemistry, and general geology.
1st call 10 February 2026 – 31 March 2026; 2nd call 15 April 2026 – 6 July 2026; 3rd call 7 July 2026 – 7 September 2026.
English language certification (for courses held in English) must be submitted by July 31st, 2026.
Arrival in Italy deadline for VISA applicants is January 31st, 2027, and verification of original documents is due by February 28th, 2027.

uncertainty_notes: Programme was activated in 2025/26 (process of accreditation indicated as ongoing on the programme page). Precise minimum CFU thresholds per scientific-disciplinary sector (SECS-P, MAT, BIO, CHIM, GEO) are defined in the official Annex/Manifesto LM-76 PDF, whose binary content could not be parsed reliably during this research — values consolidated from the apply.unimib.it programme summary. The official call PDF link cited is the 2025/26 version; the 2026/27 PDF was not yet published at the apply.unimib.it news article cited. Deadlines re-verified 2026-06-11 against apply.unimib.it 2026/27 calendar (Master track: 1st CALL 10 Feb - 31 Mar 2026; 2nd CALL 15 Apr - 6 Jul 2026 [EU + non-EU in Italy]; 3rd CALL 7 Jul - 7 Sep 2026 [EU + non-EU in Italy]). The previously inserted 4-call Nov 2025 - Sep 2026 schedule is the Bachelor/single-cycle calendar and was incorrect for this Master.
